adebayo_sanity_check.interpretation: FAIL (the EXPECTED, informative Adebayo failure) — the scrambled program ran to a DIFFERENT state (117.0/128 RAM bytes changed on average across 3 bootable randomizations), yet the Guided-BP / saliency map is BYTE-IDENTICAL (mean cosine to the real map = 1.0). This is the textbook Adebayo failure: the content-path output y=soft_ram_peek(ram,idx) is a LINEAR read whose Jacobian ∂y/∂u = e_idx is a CONSTANT one-hot, independent of the RAM values — so Guided BP / vanilla saliency is PROVABLY invariant to the program. It always points at the read index whatever the model computes ⇒ plausible-looking but model-INDEPENDENT. The intervention oracle (which re-runs the real program) does NOT fail this check — only the gradient saliency does.

extra.auc_note: deletion/insertion curves measured on the TRUE VCS by re-running the real ROM with top-Guided-BP causes occluded (deletion) / restored (insertion); every point is a genuine emulator re-run. NaN = a genuinely flat experiment.

guided_vs_vanilla.interpretation: Guided BP = vanilla |∂y/∂u| with negative-gradient suppression. On the VCS the explained output is a one-hot RAM read (a LINEAR unit, ∂y/∂u ≥ 0) and the soft emulator has NO deep ReLU stack, so the suppression removes nothing ⇒ Guided BP DEGENERATES to saliency (Δpearson = 0). The negative-suppression rule IS wired (it provably rectifies a mixed-sign surrogate — see guided_demo_rectifies); it simply has nothing to do on this substrate. This is the honest finding the DoD asks for.

harness_positive_control.interpretation: corr=1 & precision@k=1 (on a non-degenerate column) ⇒ the scoring harness rewards a faithful map; the Guided-BP numbers are then a true measurement.

extra.method_reference: Springenberg et al. 2015 (Guided Backprop / deconvnet); sanity check: Adebayo et al. 2018.
extra.output_note: POSITION/INDEX output — the §1 caveat: the pixel value comes from a discrete sprite column (round/argmax), so there is no differentiable RAM read and Guided BP VANISHES (max|attr|=0.0). The oracle has real causal signal here, so Guided BP scores near chance — the 'plausible ≠ faithful' contrast; the intervention oracle is the sole truth. Negative-gradient suppression cannot manufacture a gradient that does not exist.

sampler_on.note: naive ∂pixel/∂ram ≡ 0 (Prop. prop:zero); the bilinear sampler (tools/xai_si_gradient/si_joystick_gradient.jl) restores a real ∂pixel/∂ram[position_byte], to which the guided rule is applied. Reported naive-vs-sampler side by side. per_cause_faithfulness_null=true ⇒ the sampler's position byte is not among this game's scored candidate causes, so the per-cause Pearson is null (recorded 0.0 by the shared convention), NOT a genuine zero corr — the keystone (a non-vanishing gradient) still holds on the full 128-byte map.

extra.scales_to_cluster_via: tools/cluster/xai_*.sbatch (E0-3) — batched SOFT-STE guided gradients over outputs×causes×games on GPU; the forward is bit-exact to this map.
extra.substrate: jutari (Julia) — Zygote content-path ∂y/∂u over the RAM tape, guided rule g⊙1[g>0] (suppress −grad), + TRUE-VCS deletion/insertion re-runs (real-ROM, bit-exact oracle machinery).
